rationale: The evidence confirms a coordinated ransomware/destructive-attack preparation sequence executed within ~850ms from C:\Temp\ under corp\Administrator at High integrity: (A0) vssadmin deletes all Volume Shadow Copies to prevent recovery; (EV-002, EV-003) bcdedit disables boot status policy and Windows Recovery Environment, preventing OS-level recovery; (EV-004, EV-005) wevtutil clears the System and Security event logs to destroy forensic evidence; and (EV-001) the Security audit log was independently confirmed cleared via event code 1102. All processes share the same working directory (C:\Temp\), are launched via cmd.exe wrappers, and execute in rapid scripted succession — strongly indicating automated execution from a malicious payload. No benign explanation accounts for this combination of shadow copy deletion, recovery disablement, and multi-log wiping in a single burst.
